topic.what: Privacy risks and mitigation strategies in online propaganda detection pipelines across data collection, storage, analysis, and distribution stages
topic.how: PROMPT framework mapping risk categories to mitigation strategies via utility optimization balancing privacy gain, performance loss, and deployment cost; PRISMA-based analysis of 162 peer-reviewed studies with compliance scoring
topic.why_matters: Propaganda detection pipelines expose users to surveillance, re-identification, and regulatory non-compliance while only 18% adopt privacy-preserving technologies

characteristics.what_it_measures: Normalized cumulative privacy risk across pipeline stages (L2 norm of identity, metadata, algorithmic, and network risk vectors)
characteristics.evidence: Table 4: rident=0.241, rmeta=0.138, rbias=0.414, rnet=0.345 yielding L2 norm 0.606
characteristics.context: Across 162 surveyed studies covering C/S/A/D pipeline stages

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of propaganda detection systems incorporating privacy-preserving techniques
characteristics.evidence: Abstract: only 18% adopt privacy-preserving technologies vs 81% rely on sensitive user data

characteristics.what_it_measures: Gap between current pipeline compliance and regulatory requirements (GDPR, CCPA, EU AI Act)
characteristics.evidence: Section 7: compliance deficit of 0.306 leaves pipelines legally fragile
characteristics.context: Against thresholds tau derived from GDPR data minimization, PbD, consent requirements

characteristics.what_it_measures: Shortfall between current fairness coverage and required threshold
characteristics.context: Based on safeguard-to-risk ratio F across corpus

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 score reduction under low synthetic perturbation rate
characteristics.evidence: Abstract: with q=0.05 performance decreased by 1-2% F1
characteristics.context: Transformer fine-tuning under synthetic perturbation q

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 score reduction under high synthetic perturbation rate
characteristics.context: Transformer fine-tuning under synthetic perturbation q

characteristics.what_it_measures: Maximum accuracy drop when enforcing privacy constraints
characteristics.evidence: Conclusion: privacy constraints causing up to 22% performance degradation
